what:
OpenFn adaptor for CommCare (Dimagi). The adaptor lets OpenFn workflows read from and write to CommCare HQ.

It exposes 7 operations that you can call from a workflow step, listed in the Operations tab. Vendor homepage: https://www.commcarehq.org.
connect:
Dimagi supports API keys, HTTP Basic auth, or OAuth 2.0 for API access.

The credential schema asks for hostUrl (required), domain (required), apiKey, appId (required), username, and password. Point the credential at the base URL: https://www.commcarehq.org/a/{domain}/api/v0.5. Auth reference: https://dimagi.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/commcarepublic/pages/2143955600/Authentication.
when:
Reach for this adaptor when your workflow needs to read or write data in CommCare HQ.

Triggers haven't been catalogued yet — drive the workflow from a cron schedule or an upstream webhook in the meantime. Dimagi can push events to OpenFn via webhooks (docs: https://dimagi.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/commcarepublic/pages/2143951225/Data+Forwarding), so reactive workflows are a good fit.
wire:
A typical wiring pattern is trigger → fetch → transform → write.

Drive the workflow from a cron schedule or an upstream webhook trigger. Then chain operations from this adaptor's Operations tab to fetch or write data in Dimagi. Pass values between steps through state.data — every operation reads from and writes to job state.

To move data between systems, follow this step with another adaptor (e.g. write the fetched records into a downstream FHIR, DHIS2, or database adaptor).
scale:
When running Dimagi jobs at volume:

- Prefer Dimagi webhooks over polling — they cut latency and rate-limit pressure.
- Paginate list and search calls explicitly; never assume a single response is the full result set.
- Make jobs idempotent by keying on stable external IDs so a replay doesn't create duplicates.
- Surface 4xx/5xx responses with the vendor request ID in your logs, and treat 429s as retries with backoff.

Adapter note: Tenanted: each project (domain) has its own URL prefix /a/{domain}/.
best:
- Store Dimagi credentials in OpenFn's credential store; never inline keys or tokens in job code.
- Pin @openfn/language-commcare to v4.0.13 in package.json so an upstream change doesn't break a production workflow silently.
- Keep each step small — one logical API call per operation makes failures easier to diagnose and retry.
- Add defensive checks before transforming inbound payloads — vendor responses change between versions.
- Log enough context (resource id, run id, vendor request id) so an incident can be traced end-to-end.
